steps.title: Express s(t) in standard form
steps.content:
Since $s(t) = r(t) + 1$:
$$s(t) = 53t - 2t^2 + 1 = -2t^2 + 53t + 1$$
This is a quadratic with negative leading coefficient, so it has a maximum.

steps.title: Complete the square to find the maximum value
steps.content:
Factor out $-2$ from the first two terms:
$$s(t) = -2\left(t^2 - \frac{53}{2}t\right) + 1$$

Complete the square by adding and subtracting $\left(\frac{53}{4}\right)^2$:
$$s(t) = -2\left[\left(t - \frac{53}{4}\right)^2 - \left(\frac{53}{4}\right)^2\right] + 1$$

Distribute the $-2$:
$$s(t) = -2\left(t - \frac{53}{4}\right)^2 + 2\left(\frac{53}{4}\right)^2 + 1$$

The maximum occurs when the squared term equals zero:
$$s_{\text{max}} = 1 + 2\left(\frac{53}{4}\right)^2$$

That matches choice C — C is correct.
